institutional_unit: Cigna Corporation and Cigna Health and Life Insurance Company
decision_process: Post-service determination that a submitted claim was not medically necessary using the PxDx procedure-to-diagnosis review workflow
sampling_rule: Retrospective assessment of the workflow described in investigative reporting and the allegations and pleading-stage findings addressed in the March 2025 federal court order.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Rucker and Kelman, ProPublica and The Capitol Forum, 25 Mar. 2023: https://www.propublica.org/article/cigna-pxdx-medical-health-insurance-rejection-claims
supporting_artifacts.notes: Reporting based on internal spreadsheets and former-employee interviews; not an adjudicated factual finding.

requested_missing_artifacts: Sampled patient-file access logs showing what each medical director opened before disposition
substantive_findings.rationale: The reported workflow describes payment denials processed without opening individual patient files, which is affirmative evidence against case-specific evidence access.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Rucker and Kelman, ProPublica and The Capitol Forum, 25 Mar. 2023: https://www.propublica.org/article/cigna-pxdx-medical-health-insurance-rejection-claims
Kisting-Leung v. Cigna Corp., No. 2:23-cv-01477-DAD-CSK, E.D. Cal., 31 Mar. 2025, docket 55: https://docs.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/2%3A2023cv01477/431351/55
supporting_artifacts.notes: Procedural order on a motion to dismiss; not a merits judgment.

requested_missing_artifacts: Individual medical-director rationales showing patient-specific clinical judgment
substantive_findings.rationale: The reporting describes batch sign-off, and the court allowed a fiduciary-duty theory based on algorithmic delegation to proceed at the pleading stage.
substantive_findings.ambiguity_note: The court's order establishes procedural plausibility, not liability or a final factual finding.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Rucker and Kelman, ProPublica and The Capitol Forum, 25 Mar. 2023: https://www.propublica.org/article/cigna-pxdx-medical-health-insurance-rejection-claims

requested_missing_artifacts: Override, rejection, or stop-use records showing that medical directors exercised authority against the automated path
substantive_findings.rationale: Reported throughput controls and batch processing provide affirmative evidence that formal physician authority was not operative as individualized command in the described workflow.
substantive_findings.ambiguity_note: A second scorer could reasonably assign one based on de jure physician authority; this is retained as a designed inter-rater disagreement.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Rucker and Kelman, ProPublica and The Capitol Forum, 25 Mar. 2023: https://www.propublica.org/article/cigna-pxdx-medical-health-insurance-rejection-claims
ProPublica, Congressional Committee, Regulators Question Cigna System, 25 Apr. 2023: https://www.propublica.org/article/cigna-health-insurance-denials-pxdx-congress-investigation

requested_missing_artifacts: Population-level appeal, external-review, reversal, and interruption records specific to PxDx
substantive_findings.rationale: Formal appeal and external-review mechanisms existed, with some evidence of individual effect but no public demonstration of reliable effect at scale.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Kisting-Leung v. Cigna Corp., No. 2:23-cv-01477-DAD-CSK, E.D. Cal., 31 Mar. 2025, docket 55: https://docs.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/2%3A2023cv01477/431351/55

requested_missing_artifacts: Systemic remediation records identifying affected claims, correcting records, reimbursing loss, and assigning an owner
substantive_findings.rationale: No public artifact in the assessed record establishes systemic remediation for claims processed through PxDx.

supporting_artifacts.citation: ProPublica, Congressional Committee, Regulators Question Cigna System, 25 Apr. 2023: https://www.propublica.org/article/cigna-health-insurance-denials-pxdx-congress-investigation

requested_missing_artifacts: Authorized records revising, suspending, or retiring PxDx in response to reporting, regulatory inquiry, or litigation
substantive_findings.rationale: The assessed public record contains continued defense of the workflow and no demonstrated architecture change through the evidence cutoff.
substantive_findings.ambiguity_note: An IE finding is plausible if continued defense is treated as insufficient to establish that no internal reform occurred.

telemetry_integrity.provenance: The reporting and court order are traceable, but the core operational evidence is not a public audit record.
telemetry_integrity.completeness: Public materials expose throughput and litigation facts but do not provide a complete sample of claim files, access logs, reasons, or overrides.
telemetry_integrity.edit_authority: Cigna controlled the underlying workflow records; the public sources do not disclose edit permissions.
telemetry_integrity.independence: The decisive public record was forced by journalism, litigation, and regulatory scrutiny rather than routine independent telemetry.
telemetry_integrity.tamper_evidence: No public information establishes tamper-evident storage for the relevant review and signature records.
telemetry_integrity.retention: The public record does not establish retention coverage for all relevant PxDx artifacts.
telemetry_integrity.rationale: The public sources support several substantive findings but are insufficient to establish the completeness and independence of the underlying operational record.

summary: The assessed record indicates absent counsel, judgment, command, and reform; correction is mainly ceremonial, while repair and telemetry integrity remain insufficiently evidenced.
limitations: Investigative reporting is attributed as reporting and is not treated as an adjudicated factual record.
The March 2025 order resolves a motion to dismiss and does not establish liability or final facts.
The period start is a conservative boundary derived from the reported 2022 activity, not an exact system-launch date.
The system is described as an algorithmic claims-review workflow; this assessment does not require classifying it as machine-learning AI.
